ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken meat, coated, fried) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for spicy-seasoned chicken tenders exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of 5 spicy chicken tenders weighs approximately 150 g, equivalent to a typical fast-food meal portion as served at Jollibee Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, coated, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05062); adapted with spicy-seasoning and breading adjustment using USDA Branded Food reference for breaded spicy chicken tenders
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Jollibee-specific nutrient panel is available in approved sources. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for breaded fried chicken breast (NDB 05062) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for commercially breaded spicy chicken tenders (e.g., FDC ID 2103697 — Tyson Spicy Chicken Tenders). Sodium is elevated to reflect spicy-seasoning formulation typical of fast-food products. All values are expressed per 100 g of edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — breaded fried chicken analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee spicy chicken tenders. GI of 54 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for breaded fried chicken (GI ~54, low category), as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 21.3) / 100 ≈ 11.5, where carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 14.2 × 1.5 = 21.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~930 mg per 5-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this product, as a single 5-piece serving provides approximately 930 mg sodium — nearly 40% of the WHO-recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — which can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 54) and moderate carbohydrate load from wheat-flour breading
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this product in controlled portions, as the low GI (54) and glycemic load of approximately 11.5 per serving can cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and avoiding sugary beverages is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat, and trans fat from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this deep-fried product due to its saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g), trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), and high sodium content, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Tenders (5 pieces) is a fast-food fried chicken product served at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, including the Bugis Village outlet. Each tender is made from a strip of chicken breast or tenderloin that is marinated in a spicy seasoning blend, coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breading, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. The exterior is crunchy and well-seasoned with chilli-forward heat, while the interior remains juicy and tender. A standard serving of 5 pieces weighs approximately 150 g and provides around 395 kcal, 29.7 g protein, 20.3 g fat, and 21.3 g carbohydrate. The product is classified as ultra-processed and is halal-certified. Nutritionally, it is a moderate source of protein and B vitamins (particularly niacin and B6 from the chicken), but is high in sodium (~930 mg per serving) and contains saturated fat and trace trans fat from the frying process. The glycemic index is estimated at 54 (low category), driven by the wheat-flour breading. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, and the moderate glycemic load requires portion control for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The product contains gluten (wheat) as an allergen and may have soy cross-contact. It is dairy-free and suitable for halal dietary requirements. Common accompaniments include dipping sauces and soft drinks. This item is popular as a snack or side dish in the Singaporean fast-food context.

confidence_reason: No exact Jollibee-specific nutrient panel is available in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Food entries for breaded fried chicken breast and commercially breaded spicy chicken tenders. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05062 — breaded fried chicken breast) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food (FDC ID 2103697 — Tyson Spicy Chicken Tenders). No HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this specific Jollibee product. 2. Sodium is set at 620 mg/100 g to reflect the elevated seasoning typical of fast-food spicy chicken products; this is consistent with USDA Branded Food reference ranges for similar products (550–700 mg/100 g). 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 4. Trans fat is reported as 0.1 g/100 g, consistent with residual trans fat from commercial frying oils; this is a confirmed trace value from USDA Branded Food references. 5. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken (Atkinson et al. 2021). Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (54 × 21.3) / 100 ≈ 11.5. 6. Serving size of 150 g is estimated based on typical fast-food 5-piece chicken tender portions; Jollibee Singapore does not publish exact gram weights in approved sources. 7. Halal status confirmed from field survey cuisine tags and Jollibee Singapore's publicly known halal certification. 8. Vitamin C is 0.0 g/100 g — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for breaded fried chicken; no plant-derived vitamin C source is present in this product. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central adapted values and are not directly measured for this Jollibee product.
